text: Lesson 46
zh: 第46课

text: Hobbies
zh: 爱好

text: Who, according to the author, are 'Fortune's favoured children'?
zh: 根据作者所说,谁是'幸运女神眷顾的孩子'?

text: A gifted American psychologist has said, 'Worry is a spasm of the emotion;
zh: 一位天才的美国心理学家曾说过:'忧虑是一种情绪的痉挛;

text: the mind catches hold of something and will not let it go.'
zh: 思绪会牢牢抓住某件事,不肯放手。'

text: It is useless to argue with the mind in this condition.
zh: 在这种状态下,与思绪讲道理是没有用的。

text: The stronger the will, the more futile the task.
zh: 意志力越强,这项努力就越徒劳。

text: One can only gently insinuate something else into its convulsive grasp.
zh: 人们只能轻轻地把别的东西引入它痉挛般的抓握之中。

text: And if this something else is rightly chosen, if it is really attended by the illumination of another field of interest,
zh: 如果这个'别的东西'选择得当,如果它真的伴随着另一个兴趣领域的启发,

text: gradually, and often quite swiftly, the old undue grip relaxes and the process of recuperation and repair begins.
zh: 那么渐渐地,而且往往相当迅速地,原先那种过度的紧握就会松开,恢复和修复的过程便会开始。

text: The cultivation of a hobby and new forms of interest is therefore a policy of the first importance to a public man.
zh: 因此,培养一种爱好和新的兴趣形式,对公众人物而言是一项至关重要的策略。

text: But this is not a business that can be undertaken in a day or swiftly improvised by a mere command of the will.
zh: 但这不是一天之内就能完成的事情,也不能仅凭意志的命令临时拼凑出来。

text: The growth of alternative mental interests is a long process.
zh: 培养其他心智兴趣是一个漫长的过程。

text: The seeds must be carefully chosen; they must fall on good ground;
zh: 种子必须精心挑选,必须落在肥沃的土地上;

text: they must be sedulously tended, if the vivifying fruits are to be at hand when needed.
zh: 还必须悉心照料,这样在需要时才能获得那赋予生机的果实。

text: To be really happy and really safe, one ought to have at least two or three hobbies, and they must all be real.
zh: 要真正快乐、真正安稳,一个人至少应该有两三种爱好,而且都必须是真实的。

text: It is no use starting late in life to say: 'I will take an interest in this or that.'
zh: 到了晚年才说'我要对这个或那个产生兴趣',是没有用的。

text: Such an attempt only aggravates the strain of mental effort.
zh: 这样的尝试只会加重心智努力的负担。

text: A man may acquire great knowledge of topics unconnected with his daily work, and yet get hardly any benefit or relief.
zh: 一个人也许能对与日常工作无关的话题获得渊博的知识,却几乎得不到任何益处或解脱。

text: It is no use doing what you like; you have got to like what you do.
zh: 做你喜欢做的事没有用;你必须喜欢你所做的事。

text: Broadly speaking, human beings may be divided into three classes:
zh: 笼统地说,人类可以分为三类:

text: those who are toiled to death, those who are worried to death, and those who are bored to death.
zh: 被劳累致死的人,被忧虑致死的人,以及被无聊致死的人。

text: It is no use offering the manual labourer,
zh: 对体力劳动者来说,提议是没有用的,

text: tired out with a hard week's sweat and effort the chance of playing a game of football or baseball on Saturday afternoon.
zh: 他们经过一周辛苦的汗水和努力已经筋疲力尽,再让他们在周六下午去踢足球或打棒球是没用的。

text: It is no use inviting the politician or the professional or business man, who has been working or worrying about serious things for six days,
zh: 对政治家、专业人士或商人来说,邀请也是没有用的,他们已经连续六天为严肃的事情操劳或忧虑,

text: to work or worry about trifling things at the weekend.
zh: 却要他们在周末为琐碎小事操劳或忧虑。

text: As for the unfortunate people who can command everything they want,
zh: 至于那些不幸的人,他们能得到自己想要的一切,

text: who can gratify every caprice and lay their hands on almost every object of desire--for them a new pleasure a new excitement is only an additional satiation.
zh: 能满足每一个突发奇想,几乎能得到每一件心仪之物——对他们来说,新的快乐、新的刺激只不过是又一次的餍足。

text: In vain they rush frantically round from place to place, trying to escape from avenging boredom by mere clatter and motion.
zh: 他们徒劳地从一个地方疯狂奔向另一个地方,试图仅凭喧嚣和奔忙来逃避那报复性的无聊。

text: For them discipline in one form or another is the most hopeful path.
zh: 对他们而言,某种形式的自律才是最有希望的出路。

text: It may also be said that rational, industrious, useful human beings are divided into two classes:
zh: 也可以说,理性、勤奋、有用的人可以分为两类:

text: first, those whose work is work and whose pleasure is pleasure;
zh: 第一类,他们的工作就是工作,他们的娱乐就是娱乐;

text: and secondly, those whose work and pleasure are one.
zh: 第二类,他们的工作和娱乐合而为一。

text: Of these the former are the majority. They have their compensations.
zh: 在这两类人中,前者占多数。他们也有自己的补偿。

text: The long hours in the office or the factory bring with them as their reward,
zh: 在办公室或工厂里度过的漫长时光,给他们带来的回报,

text: not only the means of sustenance, but a keen appetite for pleasure even in its simplest and most modest forms.
zh: 不仅是维持生计的手段,还有一种对快乐的强烈渴望,哪怕是最简单、最朴素形式的快乐。

text: But Fortune's favoured children belong to the second class.
zh: 但幸运女神眷顾的孩子属于第二类。

text: Their life is a natural harmony.
zh: 他们的生活是一种自然的和谐。

text: For them the working hours are never long enough.
zh: 对他们来说,工作时间永远都不够长。

text: Each day is a holiday, and ordinary holidays, when they come, are grudged as enforced interruptions in an absorbing vocation.
zh: 每一天都是假日,而当真正的假日到来时,反倒被视为对这项令人全神贯注的事业的强制打断,令人心生不悦。

text: Yet to both classes, the need of an alternative outlook, of a change of atmosphere of a diversion of effort, is essential.
zh: 然而,对这两类人来说,拥有另一种视角、改变一下氛围、转移一下精力,都是必不可少的。

text: Indeed, it may well be that those whose work is their pleasure are those who most need the means of banishing it at intervals from their minds.
zh: 事实上,很可能正是那些以工作为乐的人,最需要不时将其从脑海中驱逐出去的方法。
